NRSV.label: New Revised Standard Version
NRSV.rights: New Revised Standard Version Bible, copyright © 1989 National Council of the Churches of Christ in the United States of America. Used by permission. All rights reserved worldwide.

text.NRSV: Paul, Silvanus, and Timothy, To the church of the Thessalonians in God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ:
text.Rotherham: Paul and Silvanus and Timothy—unto the assembly of Thessalonians, in God our Father and Lord Jesus Christ,—
text.KJV: 1:1 Paul and Siluanus, and Timotheus vnto the Church of the Thessaloniās, in God our Father, and the Lord Iesus Christ:
text.DRV: 1:1 Paul and Sylvanus and Timothee, to the church of the Thessalonians in God our Father and our Lord Jesus Christ.

text.NRSV: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
text.Rotherham: Favour unto you, and peace, from God [our] Father and Lord Jesus Christ.
text.KJV: 1:2 Grace vnto you, and peace from God our Father, and the Lorde Iesus Christ.
text.DRV: 1:2 Grace to you and peace from God our Father and our Lord Jesus Christ.

text.NRSV: We must always give thanks to God for you, brothers and sisters, as is right, because your faith is growing abundantly, and the love of every one of you for one another is increasing.
text.Rotherham: We are bound, to be giving thanks, unto God, continually, concerning you, brethren, even as it is, meet; because your faith groweth exceedingly, and the love of each one of you all one to another aboundeth,
text.KJV: 1:3 Wee are bound to thanke God alwayes for you, brethren, as it is meete, because that your faith groweth exceedingly, and the charitie of euery one of you al towards each other aboundeth:
text.DRV: 1:3 We ought to give thanks always to God for you, brethren, so as meet is, because your faith increaseth exceedingly, and the charity of every one of you aboundeth towards each other:

text.NRSV: Therefore we ourselves boast of you among the churches of God for your steadfastness and faith during all your persecutions and the afflictions that you are enduring.
text.Rotherham: So that, we ourselves, in you, are boasting, in the assemblies of God, over your endurance and faith in all your persecutions and tribulations which ye are sustaining:—
text.KJV: 1:4 So that wee our selues glorie in you in the Churches of God, for your patience and faith in all your persecutions and tribulations that yee endure.
text.DRV: 1:4 so that we ourselves also glory in you in the churches of God, for your patience, and faith in all your persecutions and tribulations, which you sustain

text.NRSV: This is evidence of the righteous judgment of God, and is intended to make you worthy of the kingdom of God, for which you are also suffering.
text.Rotherham: A proof of the righteous judgment of God, to the end ye may be counted worthy of the kingdom of God, in behalf of which ye are also suffering:—
text.KJV: 1:5 Which is a manifest token of the righteous iudgement of God, that yee may bee counted worthy of the kingdome of God, for which yee also suffer;
text.DRV: 1:5 for an example of the just judgement of God, that you may be counted worthy of the Kingdom of God, for the which also you suffer.

text.NRSV: For it is indeed just of God to repay with affliction those who afflict you,
text.Rotherham: If, at least, it is a, righteous thing with God, to recompense, affliction, unto them that afflict you,
text.KJV: 1:6 Seeing it is a righteous thing with God to recompence tribulation to them that trouble you:
text.DRV: 1:6 If yet it be just with God to repay tribulation, to them that vex you:

text.NRSV: and to give relief to the afflicted as well as to us, when the Lord Jesus is revealed from heaven with his mighty angels
text.Rotherham: And, unto you that are afflicted, release, with us,—by the revealing of the Lord Jesus from heaven, with his messengers of power,
text.KJV: 1:7 And to you who are troubled, rest with vs, when the Lord Iesus shalbe reuealed from heauen, with his mightie Angels,
text.DRV: 1:7 and to you that are vexed, rest with us in the revelation of our Lord Jesus from Heaven with the Angels of his power,

text.NRSV: in flaming fire, inflicting vengeance on those who do not know God and on those who do not obey the gospel of our Lord Jesus.
text.Rotherham: In a fiery flame; holding forth vengeance—against them that refuse to know God, and them who decline to hearken unto the glad-message of our Lord Jesus,
text.KJV: 1:8 In flaming fire, taking vengeance on them that know not God, and that obey not the Gospel of our Lorde Iesus Christ,
text.DRV: 1:8 in flame of fire, giving revenge to them that know not God, and that obey not the Gospel of our Lord Jesus Christ.

text.NRSV: These will suffer the punishment of eternal destruction, separated from the presence of the Lord and from the glory of his might,
text.Rotherham: Who, indeed, a penalty, shall pay—age-abiding destruction from the face of the Lord and from the glory of his might—
text.KJV: 1:9 Who shalbe punished with euerlasting destruction from the presence of the Lord, and from the glory of his power:
text.DRV: 1:9 Who shall suffer eternal pains in destruction, from the face of our Lord and from the glory of his power:

text.NRSV: when he comes to be glorified by his saints and to be marveled at on that day among all who have believed, because our testimony to you was believed.
text.Rotherham: Whensoever he shall come, to be made all-glorious in his saints, and to be marvelled at in all who believed,—because our witness unto you was believed,—in that day.
text.KJV: 1:10 When hee shall come to bee glorified in his Saints, and to bee admired in all them that beleeue (because our testimony among you was beleeued) in that day.
text.DRV: 1:10 when he shall come to be glorified in his saints, and to be made marvelous in all them that have believed, because our testimony concerning you was credited in that day.

text.NRSV: To this end we always pray for you, asking that our God will make you worthy of his call and will fulfill by his power every good resolve and work of faith,
text.Rotherham: Unto which end, we are also praying continually for you, that our God may count, you, worthy of your calling, and fulfil every good-pleasure of goodness and work of faith, with power,—
text.KJV: 1:11 Wherefore also we pray alwayes for you, that our God would count you worthy of this calling, and fulfill all the good pleasure of his goodnesse, and the worke of faith with power:
text.DRV: 1:11 Wherein also we pray always for you, that our God make you worthy of his vocation, and accomplish all the good pleasure of his goodness & the work of faith in power,

text.NRSV: so that the name of our Lord Jesus may be glorified in you, and you in him, according to the grace of our God and the Lord Jesus Christ.
text.Rotherham: That the name of our Lord Jesus may be made all-glorious in you, and, ye, in him, According to the favour of our God and Lord Jesus Christ.
text.KJV: 1:12 That the Name of our Lord Iesus Christ may bee glorified in you, and ye in him, according to the grace of our God, and the Lord Iesus Christ.
text.DRV: 1:12 that the name of our Lord Jesus Christ may be glorified in you, and you in him, according to the grace of our God, and of our Lord Jesus Christ.

text.NRSV: As to the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ and our being gathered together to him, we beg you, brothers and sisters,
text.Rotherham: But we request you, brethren,—in behalf of the Presence of our Lord Jesus Christ, and our gathering together unto him,
text.KJV: 2:1 Now wee beseech you, brethren, by the comming of our Lord Iesus Christ, and by our gathering together vnto him,
text.DRV: 2:1 And we desire you, brethren, by the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ, & of our congregation into him;

text.NRSV: not to be quickly shaken in mind or alarmed, either by spirit or by word or by letter, as though from us, to the effect that the day of the Lord is already here.
text.Rotherham: That ye be not quickly tossed from your mind, nor be put in alarm—either by spirit, or by discourse, or by letter as by us, as that the day of the Lord, hath set in:
text.KJV: 2:2 That yee bee not soone shaken in minde, or bee troubled, neither by spirit, nor by word, nor by letter, as from vs, as that the day of Christ is at hand,
text.DRV: 2:2 that you be not easily moved from your sense, nor be terrified, neither by spirit, nor by word, nor by epistle as sent by us, as though the day of our Lord were at hand.

text.NRSV: Let no one deceive you in any way; for that day will not come unless the rebellion comes first and the lawless one is revealed, the one destined for destruction.
text.Rotherham: That no one may cheat, you, in any one respect. Because [that day will not set in] —except the revolt come first, and there he revealed the man of lawlessness, the son of destruction,
text.KJV: 2:3 Let no man deceiue you by any meanes, for that day shall not come, except there come a falling away first, and that man of sinne bee reuealed, the sonne of perdition,
text.DRV: 2:3 Let no man seduce you by any means, for unless there come a revolt first, and the man of sin be revealed, the son of perdition,

text.NRSV: He opposes and exalts himself above every so-called god or object of worship, so that he takes his seat in the temple of God, declaring himself to be God.
text.Rotherham: The one who opposeth and exalteth himself on high against every one called God, or an object of worship; so that he, within the sanctuary of God, shall take his seat, showeth himself forth, that he is, God:—
text.KJV: 2:4 Who opposeth and exalteth himselfe aboue all that is called God, or that is worshipped: so that he as God, sitteth in the Temple of God, shewing himselfe that he is God.
text.DRV: 2:4 which is an adversary & is extolled above all that is called God, or that is worshipped, so that he sitteth in the temple of God, shewing himself as though he were God.

text.NRSV: Do you not remember that I told you these things when I was still with you?
text.Rotherham: Remember ye not, that, while I was yet with you, these very things, I was telling you?
text.KJV: 2:5 Remember yee not, that when I was yet with you, I tolde you these things?
text.DRV: 2:5 Remember you not, that when I was yet with you, I told you these things?

text.NRSV: And you know what is now restraining him, so that he may be revealed when his time comes.
text.Rotherham: And, what now restraineth, ye know, to the end he may be revealed in his own fitting time;
text.KJV: 2:6 And now yee know what withholdeth, that hee might bee reuealed in his time.
text.DRV: 2:6 And now what letteth, you know: that he may be revealed in his time.

text.NRSV: For the mystery of lawlessness is already at work, but only until the one who now restrains it is removed.
text.Rotherham: For, the secret, of lawlessness, already, is inwardly working itself,—only, until, he that restraineth at present, shall be gone, out of the midst:
text.KJV: 2:7 For the mysterie of iniquitie doth alreadie worke: onely he who now letteth, will let, vntill he be taken out of the way.
text.DRV: 2:7 (For now the mystery of iniquity worketh: only that he which now holdeth, do hold, until he be taken out of the way.)

text.NRSV: And then the lawless one will be revealed, whom the Lord Jesus will destroy with the breath of his mouth, annihilating him by the manifestation of his coming.
text.Rotherham: And, then, shall be revealed the lawless one,—whom, the Lord [Jesus], will slay with the Spirit of his mouth, and paralyse with the forthshining of his Presence:—
text.KJV: 2:8 And then shall that wicked bee reuealed, whome the Lord shall consume with the spirit of his mouth, and shall destroy with the brightnesse of his comming:
text.DRV: 2:8 And then that wicked one shall be revealed whom our Lord Jesus shall kill with the spirit of his mouth; and shall destroy with the manifestation of his advent, him,

text.NRSV: The coming of the lawless one is apparent in the working of Satan, who uses all power, signs, lying wonders,
text.Rotherham: Whose, presence, [shall be] according to an inworking of Satan, with all manner of mighty work and signs and wonders of falsehood,
text.KJV: 2:9 Euen him whose comming is after the working of Satan, with all power and signes, and lying wonders,
text.DRV: 2:9 whose coming is according to the operation of Satan, in all power, and lying signs and wonders;

text.NRSV: and every kind of wicked deception for those who are perishing, because they refused to love the truth and so be saved.
text.Rotherham: And with all manner of deceit of unrighteousness, in them who are destroying themselves, because, the love of the truth, they did not welcome, that they might be saved;—
text.KJV: 2:10 And with all deceiueablenesse of vnrighteousnesse, in them that perish: because they receiued not the loue of the trueth, that they might be saued.
text.DRV: 2:10 and in all seducing of iniquity to them that perish; for that they have not received the charity of the truth that they might be saved.

text.NRSV: For this reason God sends them a powerful delusion, leading them to believe what is false,
text.Rotherham: And, for this cause, God sendeth them an inworking of error, to the end they should believe in the falsehood,—
text.KJV: 2:11 And for this cause God shall send them strong delusion, that they should beleeue a lye:
text.DRV: 2:11 Therefore God will send them the operation of error, to believe lying:

text.NRSV: so that all who have not believed the truth but took pleasure in unrighteousness will be condemned.
text.Rotherham: In order that should be judged who would not believe in the truth, but were well-pleased with the unrighteousness.
text.KJV: 2:12 That they all might bee damned who beleeued not the trueth, but had pleasure in vnrighteousnes.
text.DRV: 2:12 that all may be judged which have not believed the truth, but have consented to iniquity.

text.NRSV: But we must always give thanks to God for you, brothers and sisters beloved by the Lord, because God chose you as the first fruits for salvation through sanctification by the Spirit and through belief in the truth.
text.Rotherham: But, we, are bound to give thanks unto God continually concerning you, brethren beloved by the Lord, for that God chose you, from the beginning, unto salvation, in sanctification of spirit and belief of truth,—
text.KJV: 2:13 But we are bound to giue thanks alway to God for you, brethren, beloued of the Lord, because God hath from the beginning chosen you to saluation, through sanctification of the spirit, and beleefe of the trueth,
text.DRV: 2:13 But we ought to give thanks to God always for you, brethren beloved of God, that he hath chosen you first-fruits unto salvation, in sanctification of spirit and faith of the truth:

text.NRSV: For this purpose he called you through our proclamation of the gospel, so that you may obtain the glory of our Lord Jesus Christ.
text.Rotherham: Unto which he called you, through means of our glad-message, unto an acquiring of the glory of our Lord Jesus Christ.
text.KJV: 2:14 Whereunto he called you by our Gospel, to the obteining of the glorie of the Lord Iesus Christ.
text.DRV: 2:14 into the which also he hath called you by our Gospel, unto the purchasing of the glory of our Lord Jesus Christ.

text.NRSV: So then, brothers and sisters, stand firm and hold fast to the traditions that you were taught by us, either by word of mouth or by our letter.
text.Rotherham: Hence, then, brethren, stand firm, and hold fast the instructions which ye were taught—whether through discourse, or through our letter.
text.KJV: 2:15 Therefore, brethren, stand fast, and hold the traditions which yee haue beene taught, whether by word or our Epistle.
text.DRV: 2:15 Therefore, brethren, stand; and hold the traditions which you have learned, whether it be by word, or by our epistle.

text.NRSV: Now may our Lord Jesus Christ himself and God our Father, who loved us and through grace gave us eternal comfort and good hope,
text.Rotherham: But may, our Lord Jesus Christ himself, and our God and Father,—Who hath loved you, and given you age-abiding consolation and good hope by favour,
text.KJV: 2:16 Now our Lorde Iesus Christ himselfe, and God euen our Father, which hath loued vs, and hath giuen vs euerlasting consolation, and good hope through grace,
text.DRV: 2:16 And our Lord Jesus Christ himself, and God and our Father which hath loved us, and hath given eternal consolation, and good hope in grace,

text.NRSV: comfort your hearts and strengthen them in every good work and word.
text.Rotherham: Console your hearts, and confirm you in every good work and word!
text.KJV: 2:17 Comfort your hearts, and stablish you in euery good word and worke.
text.DRV: 2:17 exhort your hearts and confirm you in every good work and word.

text.NRSV: Finally, brothers and sisters, pray for us, so that the word of the Lord may spread rapidly and be glorified everywhere, just as it is among you,
text.Rotherham: For the rest, brethren, be praying for us,—that, the word of the Lord, may be running, and gaining glory, according as [it did] even with you;
text.KJV: 3:1 Finally, brethren, pray for vs, that the word of the Lord may haue free course, and be glorified, euen as it is with you:
text.DRV: 3:1 For the rest, brethren, pray for us, that the word of God may have course and be glorified, as also with you:

text.NRSV: and that we may be rescued from wicked and evil people; for not all have faith.
text.Rotherham: And that we may be rescued from the presuming and wicked men; for, not all, hold the faith.
text.KJV: 3:2 And that we may bee deliuered from vnreasonable and wicked men: for all men haue not faith.
text.DRV: 3:2 and that we may be delivered from importunate and naughty men. For all men have not faith.

text.NRSV: But the Lord is faithful; he will strengthen you and guard you from the evil one.
text.Rotherham: Faithful, is the Lord,—who will confirm you, and guard you from the wicked one:
text.KJV: 3:3 But the Lord is faithfull, who shall stablish you, and keepe you from euill.
text.DRV: 3:3 But our Lord is faithful, who will confirm and keep you from evil.

text.NRSV: And we have confidence in the Lord concerning you, that you are doing and will go on doing the things that we command.
text.Rotherham: We are persuaded, however, in the Lord, as touching you,—that, what things we give in charge, Ye [both] are doing and will do;—
text.KJV: 3:4 And wee haue confidence in the Lord touching you, that yee both doe, and will doe the things which we command you.
text.DRV: 3:4 And we have confidence of you in our Lord, that the things which we command, both you do, and will do.

text.NRSV: May the Lord direct your hearts to the love of God and to the steadfastness of Christ.
text.Rotherham: But may, the Lord, guide your hearts into the love of God, and into the endurance of the Christ.
text.KJV: 3:5 And the Lord direct your hearts into the loue of God, and into the patient waiting for Christ.
text.DRV: 3:5 And our Lord direct your hearts in the charity of God, and patience of Christ.

text.NRSV: Now we command you, beloved, in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, to keep away from believers who are living in idleness and not according to the tradition that they received from us.
text.Rotherham: Howbeit, we charge you, brethren,—that, in the name of the Lord Jesus Christ, ye be withdrawing yourselves from every brother—who, in a disorderly way, doth walk, and not according to the instruction which ye received from us.
text.KJV: 3:6 Now we command you, brethren, in the Name of our Lord Iesus Christ, that ye withdraw your selues from euery brother that walketh disorderly, and not after the tradition which hee receiued of vs.
text.DRV: 3:6 And we denounce unto you, brethren, in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, that you withdraw yourselves from every brother walking inordinately, and not according to the tradition which they have received of us.

text.NRSV: For you yourselves know how you ought to imitate us; we were not idle when we were with you,
text.Rotherham: For, ye yourselves, know, how needful it is to be imitating us, in that we were not disorderly among you,
text.KJV: 3:7 For your selues know how yee ought to follow vs: for wee behaued not our selues disorderly among you,
text.DRV: 3:7 For yourselves know how you ought to imitate us: for we have not been unquiet among you:

text.NRSV: and we did not eat anyone’s bread without paying for it; but with toil and labor we worked night and day, so that we might not burden any of you.
text.Rotherham: Nor, as a free-gift, did eat, bread, from anyone, but, with toil and hardship, night and day, working,—that we might not burden any of you;—
text.KJV: 3:8 Neither did wee eate any mans bread for nought: but wrought with labour and trauaile night and day, that wee might not bee chargeable to any of you.
text.DRV: 3:8 neither have we eaten bread of any man gratis, but in labour & in toil night and day working, lest we should burden any of you.

text.NRSV: This was not because we do not have that right, but in order to give you an example to imitate.
text.Rotherham: Not because we have not authority, but that, ourselves, as an ensample, we might hold forth unto you,—to the end ye might he imitating us.
text.KJV: 3:9 Not because we haue not power, but to make our selues an ensample vnto you to follow vs.
text.DRV: 3:9 Not as though we had not authority: but that we might give ourselves a pattern unto you for to imitate us.

text.NRSV: For even when we were with you, we gave you this command: Anyone unwilling to work should not eat.
text.Rotherham: For, even when we were with you, this, were we giving in charge unto you,—that, if any will not work, neither let him eat!
text.KJV: 3:10 For euen when wee were with you, this wee commanded you, that if any would not worke, neither should he eate.
text.DRV: 3:10 For also when we were with you, this we denounced to you, that if any will not work, neither let him eat.

text.NRSV: For we hear that some of you are living in idleness, mere busybodies, not doing any work.
text.Rotherham: We hear, in fact, of some who are walking among you in a disorderly way, at nothing, working, yet too busily working!
text.KJV: 3:11 For we heare that there are some which walke among you disorderly, working not at all, but are busi-bodies.
text.DRV: 3:11 For we have heard of certain among you that walk unquietly, working nothing, but curiously meddling.

text.NRSV: Now such persons we command and exhort in the Lord Jesus Christ to do their work quietly and to earn their own living.
text.Rotherham: Now, such as these, we charge and exhort in the Lord Jesus Christ,—that, with quietness, working, their own bread, they be eating.
text.KJV: 3:12 Now them that are such, we command, and exhort by our Lord Iesus Christ, that with quietnesse they worke, and eat their owne bread.
text.DRV: 3:12 And to them that be such we denounce, & beseech them in our Lord Jesus Christ, that working with silence, they eat their own bread.

text.NRSV: Brothers and sisters, do not be weary in doing what is right.
text.Rotherham: But, ye, brethren, be not weary in well-doing;—
text.KJV: 3:13 But ye, brethren, be not wearie in well doing.
text.DRV: 3:13 But you, brethren faint not well-doing.

text.NRSV: Take note of those who do not obey what we say in this letter; have nothing to do with them, so that they may be ashamed.
text.Rotherham: And, if anyone be not giving ear unto our word through means of this letter, on this one, set a mark—not to be mixing yourselves up with him,—that he may be reproved;
text.KJV: 3:14 And if any man obey not our word, by this Epistle note that man, and haue no company with him, that he may be ashamed,
text.DRV: 3:14 And if any obey not our word, note him by an epistle:

text.NRSV: Do not regard them as enemies, but warn them as believers.
text.Rotherham: And, not as an enemy, be esteeming him, but be admonishing him, as a brother.
text.KJV: 3:15 Yet count him not as an enemie, but admonish him as a brother.
text.DRV: 3:15 and do not company with him, that he may be confounded: and do not esteem him as an enemy, but admonish him as a brother.

text.NRSV: Now may the Lord of peace himself give you peace at all times in all ways. The Lord be with all of you.
text.Rotherham: But may, the Lord of peace himself, give you peace, always, in every way. The Lord, be with you all.
text.KJV: 3:16 Now the Lord of peace himselfe, giue you peace alwayes, by all meanes. The Lord be with you all.
text.DRV: 3:16 And the Lord of peace himself give you everlasting peace in every place. Our Lord be with you all.

text.NRSV: I, Paul, write this greeting with my own hand. This is the mark in every letter of mine; it is the way I write.
text.Rotherham: The salutation of Paul—with my own hand, which is a sign in every letter: thus, I write.
text.KJV: 3:17 The salutation of Paul, with mine owne hand, which is the token in euery Epistle: so I write.
text.DRV: 3:17 The salutation, with mine own hand, Paul's: which is a sign in every epistle. So I write.

text.NRSV: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with all of you.
text.Rotherham: The favour of our Lord Jesus Christ, be with you all.
text.KJV: 3:18 The grace of our Lord Iesus Christ be with you all, Amen. The second Epistle to the Thessalonians was written from Athens.
text.DRV: 3:18 The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you all. Amen.
